notes: The AskUserQuestion assertion is non-discriminating for with_skill (always passes) since the skill explicitly instructs it. It is discriminating for baselines.

The baseline for eval 2 (plugin system) used 28K tokens — nearly 2x the with-skill version — because it explored the entire codebase. Despite this extra context, it still missed interview structure elements (checkpoint, one-at-a-time).

The biggest quality delta is on eval 1 (dotfiles): with-skill questions paint vivid scenarios while baseline asks generic questions. The skill's emphasis on non-obvious, scenario-based questioning had the clearest effect here.
The mid-interview checkpoint (eval 2, assertion 5) is a skill-specific behavior that never appears in baselines — a genuine structural improvement in interview flow.
